title: UX Investigation Method
purpose: Defines the ordered, repeatable method agents use to move from source inventory to exhaustive current UX capture without skipping hidden branches or inventing desired behavior.

orderedMethod.title: Source Route Inventory
orderedMethod.summary: Generate or manually create a deterministic inventory of compiled frontend routes, route patterns, layout chains, and excluded route-like folders.

orderedMethod.title: Route And Surface Decomposition
orderedMethod.summary: Break each route into layouts, shells, screens, sections, subscreens, dialogs, drawers, forms, lists, cards, action groups, and supporting controls.

orderedMethod.title: Runtime Observation
orderedMethod.summary: Manually observe the route and surface behavior users can see, including states, transitions, validation, recovery, navigation, permissions, and content behavior.

orderedMethod.title: Source Traversal For Hidden Branches
orderedMethod.summary: Inspect source for conditional UX that may not appear in a single runtime pass, including auth, role gates, redirects, readiness, empty states, error branches, feature flags, and deep-link behavior.

orderedMethod.title: UX Hierarchy Classification
orderedMethod.summary: Assign discovered behavior to feature, subfeature, implied subfeature, journey, flow, subflow, requirement, observable scenario, acceptance criterion, or pending decision.

orderedMethod.title: Current UX Requirement Capture
orderedMethod.summary: Convert meaningful observed behavior into current UX requirement items without treating current behavior as desired behavior.

orderedMethod.title: Observable Scenario Capture
orderedMethod.summary: Capture loading, empty, error, invalid, dirty, stale, auth, permission, success, restore, and recovery situations when they affect user-visible behavior.

orderedMethod.title: Coverage Indexing
orderedMethod.summary: Create coverage rows for routes, surfaces, hidden lanes, requirements, scenarios, and acceptance criteria so missing branches are visible.

orderedMethod.title: Pending Decision Routing
orderedMethod.summary: Route unresolved product intent, desired behavior, ownership, missing expected basics, suspected accidental behavior, and proof gaps into explicit pending decisions.

rules: Do not start runtime observation before the route/source inventory exists.
Do not treat route discovery as complete when dynamic routes or excluded underscore folders have not been accounted for.
Do not author desired UX while current UX is still being discovered unless the user explicitly requests desired UX decisions.
Continue capturing known current UX even when some branches remain pending or unknown.
Every unresolved branch must be represented as coverage, pending decision, or intentionally omitted with rationale.
